Item: I trust the AI to provide me information that helps me manage my psychological challenges
I trust the AI to help me clarify my thoughts about a difficult situation
I trust that what the AI tells me about psychological concepts and processes is accurate and reliable
I can rely on the AI to make sense of the perspective of someone I am having difficulties to understand
Using the AI enables me to see how my experiences fit into what is common for others
I trust the AI to understand my specific needs when I ask for support
I trust the AI to give me advice that is well-tailored to my specific situation
I trust that the more information the AI has about me, the better it can support me
I trust the AI to help me gain new insights into my issues
I trust the AI to suggest helpful strategies based on my situation
I trust the AI to reflect the feelings I share in a way that feels accurate
I trust the AI to make me feel heard and validated
The AI helps me regulate my emotions when I feel distressed
I trust the AI to help me feel more confident in my ability to handle a difficult situation
I trust the AI to adjust its responses appropriately as my emotions change during our conversation
I feel comfortable sharing my private and sensitive information with the AI
I feel safe opening up to the AI about anything that is on my mind
I trust that the AI responses are free from harmful social stereotypes
I trust the AI to provide me with a space where I can express myself freely
I trust the AI to respond to me in ways that respect who I am
I am concerned that the AI may not be able to respond appropriately to a mental health crisis (e.g., feeling hopeless, stuck, or wanting to self-harm)
Using the AI as my primary source of psychological support would be risky
I am concerned that the AI does not sufficiently highlight its limitations as a mental health tool
I doubt that the AI can provide sufficient support for complex mental health conditions
I believe the AI may provide inaccurate or misleading mental health information
I am concerned that the AI company may use my data in ways I do not approve of
I am skeptical that the AI company ensures the AI is safe to use for mental health support
I am concerned that the values of the AI company do not sufficiently align with my own
I am concerned that the AI company treats my wellbeing as secondary to their commercial interests
I am concerned that the way the AI communicates may lead me to trust it more than I should
It is up to me to guide the AI to give responses that meet my needs
I trust AI responses less as conversations get longer and the AI starts to 'drift' (e.g., speaks repetitively or less consistently)
When in doubt, I would trust my own experience over what the AI tells me
I feel the need to verify the AI's sources when the information is important
The AI cannot replace human connections for emotional support
Even though the AI sounds human, our interactions lack the shared experience of human connections
The emotional support the AI can offer is limited by its lack of embodied feelings and shared presence
While the AI uses empathic language, it cannot care about my wellbeing as a good friend would
Even though I can talk with the AI about my feelings, it does not experience emotions the way I do
